verdict: The chart is technically on-beat, but it is highly uncomfortable to play due to repetitive spin patterns, sudden 16th-note jumps, and forced double-taps.

issues.problem: Systematic 16th-note jacks into jumps (L followed immediately by JUMP LR) force an extremely rapid, uncomfortable double-tap on the left foot while trying to land a jump.
The continuous D -> U -> R -> L -> D -> U -> R stream creates a spin pattern that forces the player to either turn completely away from the screen or awkwardly double-step.
Landing a JUMP LR and immediately transitioning into 16th-note taps (D then U) is physically jarring and lacks flow.
Isolated 16th-note steps create random gallop rhythms that do not match the straight 4/4 house beat of the song.
